title: Post-hackathon: validate the OSR overshoot-reversion edge on external real L2 (Tardis crypto)
source: External-L2 data-source scan 2026-06-16 (docs/submission/external_l2_data_sources_2026-06-16.md), CEO-requested after the OSR/L2 Jun-18 gate converged NO-GO.
why: OSR could not be MEASURED on the 27-day xSyphon drop (event-starved, <=8 trades/trial; window too short for |z|>=2 overshoots to clear N>=300 after the two registered gates). The scan found external real L2 that CAN provide the history: Tardis.dev offers top-5 AND top-25 tick order-book snapshots, BTC/ETH since 2019, SOL since 2020 (3-7yr -> clears the N>=300 floor), with a FREE 1st-of-month sample and ~$300 min for the full pull; its book_snapshot_5 schema maps near-1:1 to pipeline/l2_adapter.py. CoinAPI is a backup. FX/metals real depth is locked behind enterprise LMAX (contact-sales); cheap FX sources (Dukascopy/HistData/TrueFX) are top-of-book only.
value_rationale: Answers the open research question 'does the overshoot-reversion microstructure concept have a REAL edge?' that the hackathon data could not. Medium value: research-grade only.
scope: Free Tardis monthly sample -> wire/validate the l2_adapter mapping (book_snapshot_5 -> bid_px/sz_1..5, ask_px/sz_1..5) at zero cost.
Bounded ~1-month spike on BTC/ETH/SOL top-5 to check event count + a first OSR signal read (pre-registered falsification bar BEFORE evaluation, per the existing apparatus).
If the spike clears the bar, ~$300 multi-year pull -> full pre-registered walk-forward/CPCV validation with DSR deflation.
FX/metals: real depth only via enterprise LMAX -> out of scope unless funded.
recommendation: POST-HACKATHON, de-prioritised. Start with the free Tardis sample (zero cost). Do NOT pursue before Jun-21 — it cannot yield a deployable leg.
out_of_scope: Any Jun-21 live-week deployment. The competition's live MT5 venue is a SYNTHETIC pricer ladder (B0188: live<->backtest depth parity FALSE), so even a real-L2-validated edge would NOT transfer to the contest -> this is research-grade, never a live leg, unless the live venue changes to real depth.

external_refs.label: External L2 source scan (12 vendors, ranked)

history.reason: Spawned from the CEO-requested external-L2 data scan; preserves the OSR research line post-hackathon (Tardis crypto), with the B0188 transfer caveat as out-of-scope.
CEO: start B0190. First slice = Tardis book_snapshot_5 -> wide-CSV adapter, TDD vs synthetic fixture (no credentials needed); actual data pull gated on a free Tardis API key (CEO action).

Tardis book_snapshot_5 -> wide-CSV adapter (pipeline/tardis_adapter.py) + TDD suite (tests/test_tardis_adapter.py, 11 tests) green vs synthetic fixture; reuses l2_adapter wide-schema/colspec/variant; round-trips through B0163 ticks loader. Data pull still gated on a free Tardis API key (CEO action).

Organizer (Duncan 2026-06-16): backtest depth is static by design -> external real L2 (Tardis) is now the ONLY path to know if an OSR microstructure edge exists at all. AND "live liquidity/execution may change once real trading starts" upgrades B0190 from pure-academic toward could-inform-a-future-live-capability IF live depth proves real at launch. Strengthens the B0190 rationale.

Duncan 2026-06-16: the CONTEST env will never simulate participant-driven order flow -> an OSR edge found on external real-market L2 (Tardis) can NEVER transfer to this contest. B0190 reverts to PURE RESEARCH (does a real-market microstructure edge exist?), not a future-contest capability.
